context:
Page 27, Section 7 (Conclusions)

"Nevertheless, there are many other unresolved stability issues in NEPs. For instance, is the I-property also necessary for the isolated calmness of \(S_{KKT}\)? In the context of NLP, the answer is positive, where the I-property actually reduces to the SOSC (Dontchev and Rockafellar [9]) under the SONC for optimality."
title: Necessity of the I-property for isolated calmness of the KKT solution mapping in Nash equilibrium problems
latex:
Consider a Nash equilibrium problem (NEP) with \(N\) players. For each player \(k\in\{1,\dots,N\}\), let the decision variable be \(x_k\in\mathbb{R}^{n_k}\) and denote \(x=(x_1,\dots,x_N)\in\mathbb{R}^n\) with \(n=\sum_{k=1}^N n_k\). Let the canonical perturbation parameter be \(p=(p_1,\dots,p_N)\) where \(p_k=(u_k,v_k,w_k)\in\mathbb{R}^{m_k}\times\mathbb{R}^{n_k}\times\mathbb{R}^{d_k}\). For fixed \(x_{-k}\) and \(p_k\), player \(k\) solves
\[
\min_{x_k\in\mathbb{R}^{n_k}}\ f_k(x_k,x_{-k};w_k)-\langle v_k,x_k\rangle\quad\text{s.t.}\quad g_i^k(x_k;w_k)=u_i^k\ (i=1,\dots,s_k),\ \ g_j^k(x_k;w_k)\le u_j^k\ (j=s_k+1,\dots,m_k),
\]
where \(f_k\) and \(g_i^k\) are twice continuously differentiable in \(x_k\). Define the (unperturbed) Lagrangian for player \(k\) by
\[
L_k(x_k,x_{-k},\lambda_k;w_k)=f_k(x_k,x_{-k};w_k)+\sum_{i=1}^{m_k}\lambda_i^k\, g_i^k(x_k;w_k),
\]
with multipliers \(\lambda_k\in\mathbb{R}^{m_k}\) satisfying \(\lambda_j^k\ge 0\) for inequality indices \(j>s_k\). Let \(S_{\mathrm{KKT}}:p\rightrightarrows (x,\lambda)\) denote the KKT solution mapping of the NEP, i.e., \((x,\lambda)\in S_{\mathrm{KKT}}(p)\) if and only if for each \(k\), the KKT conditions of player \(k\)'s problem at \((x_k,x_{-k})\) hold at \(p_k\).

Fix \((\bar p,\bar x,\bar\lambda)\in\mathrm{gph}\,S_{\mathrm{KKT}}\). For each player \(k\), define the active sets at \((\bar p,\bar x,\bar\lambda)\):
- \(I_1^k:=\{1,\dots,s_k\}\cup\{j\in\{s_k+1,\dots,m_k\}:\ \bar\lambda_j^k>0\ \text{and}\ g_j^k(\bar x_k;\bar w_k)=\bar u_j^k\}\),
- \(I_2^k:=\{j\in\{s_k+1,\dots,m_k\}:\ \bar\lambda_j^k=0\ \text{and}\ g_j^k(\bar x_k;\bar w_k)=\bar u_j^k\}\).
Let \(I_1:=\bigcup_{k=1}^N I_1^k\) and \(I_2:=\bigcup_{k=1}^N I_2^k\). Define the cone
\[
K(I_1,I_2):=\Big\{y=(y_1,\dots,y_N)\in\mathbb{R}^n:\ \nabla_{x_k}g_i^k(\bar x_k;\bar w_k)^T y_k=0\ \forall i\in I_1^k,\ \nabla_{x_k}g_i^k(\bar x_k;\bar w_k)^T y_k\le 0\ \forall i\in I_2^k\Big\}.
\]
Define the I-property at \((\bar p,\bar x,\bar\lambda)\) as:
\[
\text{for any }y\in K(I_1,I_2),\ \Big(\sum_{i=1}^N y_k^T\nabla^2_{x_k x_i}L_k(\bar x_k,\bar x_{-k},\bar\lambda_k;\bar w_k)\,y_i=0\ \text{for all }k=1,\dots,N\Big)\ \Longrightarrow\ y=0.
\]
The problem is to determine whether this I-property is also necessary for isolated calmness of \(S_{\mathrm{KKT}}\) at \(\bar p\) for \((\bar x,\bar\lambda)\).

Formally: determine whether the following implication holds in general for NEPs:
\[
S_{\mathrm{KKT}}\ \text{is isolated calm at }\bar p\ \text{for }(\bar x,\bar\lambda)\ \Longrightarrow\ \text{the I-property holds at }(\bar p,\bar x,\bar\lambda)\ \text{on }K(I_1,I_2).
\]
If the implication fails, characterize additional assumptions under which it becomes true, or provide a counterexample.
open_problem_quote: "Nevertheless, there are many other unresolved stability issues in NEPs. For instance, is the I-property also necessary for the isolated calmness of \(S_{KKT}\)? In the context of NLP, the answer is positive, where the I-property actually reduces to the SOSC (Dontchev and Rockafellar [9]) under the SONC for optimality."

verification.notice: This entry was generated from the paper text only. It has not been independently verified as still open. If you believe the question is solved or incorrectly stated, please contact the maintainers.
